fedEvents.title: Building-Products Distributor QXO Launches Hostile Bid for Beacon
fedEvents.content: QXO is taking its offer directly to shareholders after being rebuffed on several occasions.

fedEvents.title: Chicago Fed President Goolsbee says inflation is too high; Williams sees price pressures easing
fedEvents.content: In a live CNBC interview from his home district, Goolsbee declined to speculate on where he thinks interest rates are headed.

fedEvents.title: Judge blocks Trump's rule limiting federal student loans for certain grad school borrowers
fedEvents.content: A federal judge temporarily blocked Trump's limits on how much certain graduate students can borrow. That policy was set to go into effect July 1.

fedEvents.title: Core inflation rate hit 3.4% in May, highest since October 2023, Fed’s preferred gauge shows
fedEvents.content: The personal consumption expenditures price index was expected to show a 4.1% annual increase.

items.title: The Wall Street equivalent of roster-cut day happens Friday. It’ll be one of the biggest volume days of the year.
items.content: FTSE Russell is undertaking its semi-annual rebalancing — what the index provider calls its reconstitution — and the size of the undertaking could impact markets, say analysts.

items.title: SpaceX FOMO is officially over. Space stocks across the board are getting punished.
items.content: Investors seem to be having second thoughts about the lofty valuations in the sector, an analyst said.

items.title: SpaceX stock is a terrible buy — what that actually means for the bull market
items.content: Overhyped IPOs rarely make you money in the short term. That doesn’t mean it’s time to sell your stocks..

items.title: A world rejecting OPEC controls could usher in oil below $50 a barrel
items.content: Iraq’s hint that it could exit OPEC is yet another source of potential tumult for the global oil market in 2026.

items.title: ‘I have full guardianship’: My son has special needs. If I buy him a condo, will that hurt his benefits?
items.content: “Another option would be for me to purchase the condo myself and have him pay me rent.”

items.title: The S&P 500 is at a critical crossroads. A break lower could signal more losses ahead.
items.content: The S&P 500 on Thursday finished right on the cusp of a critical support line that, if broken, could portend more losses for stocks in the coming days and weeks.

items.title: After losing my son, would it be a mistake to sell my recently purchased house and move to my hometown?
items.content: “I raised my children there as a single mother, and it was the last home my son lived in before he passed away unexpectedly.”

items.title: Microsoft’s stock is suffering a historic June rout as investors balk at heavy spending
items.content: Those who owned Microsoft’s stock for the free-cash-flow profile now “are being asked to underwrite a capital-intensity cycle,” says an analyst.

items.title: Japanese stocks are on fire. Here’s what’s driving the hot streak.
items.content: Japanese equities have hit all-time highs at a pace not seen since 1989.

items.title: Apple’s MacBooks and iPads are getting more expensive as the memory crunch deepens
items.content: Apple increased prices on select MacBooks and iPads Thursday morning on the heels of a blowout Micron earnings report.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.content: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.content: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.content: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
items.content: Gain insight on Experian, SoFi Technologies and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

items.title: Arabica Coffee Prices Hit Record on U.S., Colombia Tariff Spat
items.content: Arabica coffee prices hit a record level, as traders digested the withdrawal of President Trump’s threats to impose tariffs and economic sanctions on Colombia.

items.title: Swiss franc, Japanese yen Rise as DeepSeek News Boosts Safe Havens
items.content: The Yen and Swiss Franc were stronger against the dollar as investors sought safe havens after Chinese start-up DeepSeek’s new AI model hit U.S. tech stocks.

items.title: Natural Gas Falls on Shifting Weather Forecasts
items.content: U.S. natural gas futures fell after weekend updates took some of the chill out of early February weather forecasts.

items.content: QXO is taking its offer directly to shareholders after being rebuffed on several occasions.

items.title: Natural-Gas Firm Diversified Energy Strikes Deal for Permian Basin Player

items.title: What's New This Tax Season That Can Save You Money

items.title: Chinese Investors With Few Options Turn to Dividends
items.content: Companies with good payouts are now the hottest bet in China markets.

items.title: U.S. Treasury Yields Fall But Direction for Long-End Yields Still Seen Upward
items.content: The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield fell and ING said the the long end of the Treasury curve will continue trading at higher yields even as Trump hasn’t delivered anything to shock markets so far.

items.title: Russia's Crude Exports Fairly Stable Despite U.S. Sanctions, Goldman Says
items.content: Oil edged lower in early trade as Goldman Sachs said the latest round of U.S. sanctions against Russia haven’t had a significant impact on the country’s oil exports.

items.title: The Extra Reward for Owning Stocks Over Bonds Has Disappeared
items.content: There is little sign of crimped demand for equities among individual investors, who remain bullish after two years of blockbuster gains.

items.content: Recent court cases reveal how middlemen are facilitating a booming trade, eluding a crackdown.

items.content: The story of home affordability in the U.S. told from a single front porch.

items.content: The president has told Bank of America and JPMorgan Chase to stop cutting conservatives off from doing business.

items.content: The LinkedIn co-founder is starting Manas AI with “The Emperor of All Maladies” author Siddhartha Mukherjee.

items.content: Apps can add flexibility and freshness to your routine. Just try not to get injured.

items.title: Canada to Review Amazon Contracts After Quebec Layoffs
items.content: Canada has warned Amazon.com that it is reviewing business ties with the company’s cloud-computing unit following Amazon’s decision to shut down its warehouses in Quebec, leading to the layoff of 1,700 workers.

items.content: The platform has provided a surge in discovery and revenue for companies that may not be able to replicate it elsewhere.

items.title: What Happens Now That TikTok's Gone Dark?
items.content: The company told users the app isn’t available and told them to stay tuned. Trump said he will likely intervene.

items.content: Oura’s smart ring measures heart health and can predict when you may be getting sick

items.title: SoftBank plunges 12%, SK Hynix slides 8% as Asia tech rout tracks declines in the U.S.

items.title: U.S.-Iran peace deal grants access to Tehran's nuclear sites, UN watchdog says
items.content: "The technical work has started, and we hope to be there soon," IAEA chief Rafael Grossi said at a news conference in Japan on Friday.

items.title: UN agency pauses Hormuz ship evacuation plan after first vessel attack under peace deal
items.content: The pause follows a container ship being struck near the coast of Oman on Thursday, with a U.S. official saying that Iran was responsible for the attack.

items.title: NBA targets Asian resurgence with technology and talent push
items.content: Southeast Asia has emerged as one of the NBA's fastest-growing markets for fan engagement, making the region a focal point for both player development and business expansion.

items.title: Oil slides 2% as markets look past fresh Iran tensions and focus on supply outlook
items.content: Oil extended declines on Friday, with markets focused any further breakthroughs in the Middle East conflict, while digesting a flurry of news from the region.

items.title: Airwallex hits $11 billion valuation with $320 million raise as fintech pushes into finance run by AI agents
items.content: Airwallex raised $320 million to build the financial infrastructure powering a world where AI agents handle business payments and finance.

items.title: Anthropic's latest hiring spree reveals where it's building AI data centers next
items.content: The AI lab is hiring for AI data center roles in Australia and Japan as it rushes to expand compute capacity overseas.

items.title: One of India's largest gold exporters paid its managing director just $180 a month, investigators say in accounting probe
items.content: Indian authorities have found accounting discrepancies in books of Indian gold company Rajesh Exports, and owner of Swiss refiner Valcambi.

items.title: ON Semiconductor strikes $7 billion deal for Synaptics in physical AI push
items.content: ON Semiconductor said the deal bumps up its total addressable market by $30 billion, to $243 billion by 2030.

items.title: Iran behind attack on cargo vessel near Oman in Strait of Hormuz, U.S. official tells MS NOW
items.content: "The only crop we're harvesting is what you planted: decades of mistrust," Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said in a social media post.

items.title: Luxury spending now driven by experiences and 'inheritourism'
items.content: While luxury goods sales are expected to grow between 1% and 4%, experiences are on track for growth of between 3% and 7% this year, according to a new report.

items.title: Chicago Fed President Goolsbee says inflation is too high; Williams sees price pressures easing
items.content: In a live CNBC interview from his home district, Goolsbee declined to speculate on where he thinks interest rates are headed.

items.title: Democrats probe Trump's troubled $16 million reflecting pool renovation
items.content: President Donald Trump has attempted to spruce up Washington in advance of the country's 250th anniversary.

items.title: Trump keeps turning Republican wins into loyalty tests — and political liabilities
items.content: President Donald Trump’s moves on housing, FISA, Iran and Washington projects are creating new headaches for Republicans trying to show voters they can govern.

items.title: Apple stock gets slammed on bigger Mac, iPad price hikes. Why it can weather the storm
items.content: The stock had its worst session in more than a year as management made its first official move to pass higher memory costs onto consumers.

items.title: Supreme Court limits Roundup cancer suits against Bayer's Monsanto
items.content: Glyphosate, used in Roundup weedkiller, is the most commonly utilized herbicide in agriculture, and it has long been linked to cancer claims.

items.title: Judge says lawsuit against Trump DOJ 'anti-weaponization' fund will proceed
items.content: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche originally set up the fund as part of a settlement of a lawsuit by President Donald Trump against the IRS.

items.title: GM reveals 2027 GMC Sierra pickup with new V-8 engines, redesigned styling
items.content: The new GMC Sierra trucks are crucial to GM's sales and earnings, especially highly profitable luxury Denali and off-road AT4 models.

items.title: As Social Security faces trust fund depletion, some Washington lawmakers call for taxing high earners
items.content: High earners may only contribute Social Security payroll taxes for part of the year. As the program faces funding woes, some lawmakers say that should change.

items.title: Microsoft lifts price of Xbox consoles due to soaring component costs
items.content: Microsoft said it's increasing the price of Xbox game consoles after Apple announced price hikes for MacBooks and iPads.

items.title: JPMorgan names Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh co-presidents as longtime exec Marianne Lake exits
items.content: The moves would appear to make Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh the most obvious possible successors to JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon.

items.title: Judge blocks Trump's rule limiting federal student loans for certain grad school borrowers
items.content: A federal judge temporarily blocked Trump's limits on how much certain graduate students can borrow. That policy was set to go into effect July 1.

items.title: Core inflation rate hit 3.4% in May, highest since October 2023, Fed’s preferred gauge shows
items.content: The personal consumption expenditures price index was expected to show a 4.1% annual increase.

items.title: Bond ETF flows surge in hunt for yield: 'Market sniffing out something here,' says BlackRock exec
items.content: Bond investors are abandoning aggregate benchmarks in favor of a broad mix of fixed-income investments to maximize yield with the stock market on edge.

items.title: Wendy's turns lower as meme rally fails to extend to a second day
items.content: The stock's volatile run appeared largely disconnected from company fundamentals and instead reflected a burst of social media enthusiasm.

items.title: Chan Zuckerberg Initiative's Biohub to open new rare disease funding round
items.content: Chan Zuckerberg Initiative's Biohub will offer new grants to the rare disease community while expanding its AI drug repurposing partnership with Every Cure.

items.title: Chevron says no quick fix for gas prices as Trump takes on Big Oil: ‘It’s going to take time’
items.content: Her comments come shortly after President Donald Trump ordered an investigation into Big Oil, accusing them of "gouging" consumers.

items.title: TikTok and YouTube are reinventing sports viewership. Broadcasters are taking note
items.content: Leagues, teams and media networks are increasingly meeting younger fans on social media as platforms like TikTok, Roblox and YouTube capture most of their time.

items.title: GameStop’s CEO just sacrificed a $35 billion pay package. Here’s how it could impact his effort to buy eBay
items.content: Ryan Cohen has shared few details on how he'll move forward with his $56 billion offer for eBay since the company rejected the proposal in May.

items.title: Trump pledges rapid U.S. response for Venezuela after historic earthquakes kill hundreds
items.content: The swift U.S. offer of assistance reflects a degree of diplomatic realignment between the Trump administration and the Venezuelan interim government.
